universalEdges.evidence: Budgets allocate labor cost for every trade on the project
Budgets designate spend for every material category
Budgets reserve funding for any required equipment
Budget line items carry cost estimates for every trade's work
Budget line items derive values from takeoff quantities of every material
Cost codes classify labor hours and dollars for every trade
Cost codes group purchase costs for every material category
Cost codes classify rental charges for every equipment type
WBS codes organize every trade's scope in the work breakdown
WBS codes link every material's cost to deliverables in the breakdown
Budget codes aggregate costs for every trade
Budget codes label procurement for every material
Subcontracts engage labor from every trade discipline
Purchase orders procure every material category
Purchase orders rent any equipment type
Commitment line items define labor scope for any trade
Commitment line items break down quantities for any material
Commitment line items specify rental terms for any equipment
Databricks Q4: change events span 30 distinct reasons across all trade and material categories
Change events capture price adjustments for any material
CE line items recalculate cost for changed conditions affecting any trade
CE line items modify quantities for any material
PCOs can request additional work by any trade
PCOs can estimate added cost for any material
CCOs approve extra scope for any trade — Databricks: 250K design CCOs across 4,200 cos
CCOs lock in cost of additional material for any category
CCO line items charge for incremental labor by any trade
CCO line items carry unit prices for any additional material
Owner COs pass through cost for work by any trade
Owner COs recover material cost increases for any material

Budget modifications move dollars to cover any material cost overrun
Direct costs record charges for any trade on T&M basis
Direct costs capture purchases of any material outside commitments
Direct costs record rental charges for any equipment
DC line items document receipt of any material delivery
DC line items bill mobilization of any equipment
Owner invoices draw against contract value for any trade's work
Owner invoices include installed value of any material
Sub invoices pay for any trade's work-in-place
Sub invoices reimburse installed value of any material
Retainage withheld from every trade's earned payment
Retainage defers percentage of payment for any material
Payments disburse earned funds to any trade
Payments close out balances for any material procurement
Lien waivers release supplier lien rights on any delivered material
SOV itemizes costs for any trade into billable line items
SOV assigns dollar values to any material line for billing
Allowances reserve provisional amounts for any material category
Contingency provides cost buffer for labor overruns by any trade
Contingency protects against price increases for any material
Cost forecasts estimate total spend for every resource category
Cost forecasts predict cumulative rental cost for any equipment
Cash flow projects payment milestones for any material
Budget variance highlights overruns for any trade
Cost growth quantifies price increases for any material
CPI compares earned value to actual cost for any trade
Committed cost monitors contractual dollars obligated to any trade
Change order rate counts frequency of changes for any trade
Contingency burn tracks how overruns on any material consume reserves
Invoice processing time applies to invoices from any trade

verbGlossary.PROVISIONS HEADCOUNT FOR: {source} allocates labor cost allowance to cover {target}'s planned crew size
verbGlossary.EARMARKS SPEND FOR: {source} designates a dollar amount for procurement of {target} category
verbGlossary.RESERVES CAPACITY FOR: {target} sets aside funding to guarantee availability of {source}
verbGlossary.PRICED FOR: {target} carries a unit-rate cost estimate for {source}'s work
verbGlossary.QUANTITIES FROM: {source} derives its dollar value from takeoff quantities of {target}
verbGlossary.CLASSIFIES LABOR OF: {source} organizes {target}'s labor hours and dollars into a standard accounting category
verbGlossary.CATEGORIZES MATERIAL OF: {source} groups {target}'s purchase and delivery costs under a standard classification
verbGlossary.CATEGORIZES RENTAL OF: {source} assigns {target} rental charges to a standard cost classification
verbGlossary.STRUCTURES WORK OF: {target} places {source}'s scope within the project's hierarchical work breakdown
verbGlossary.MAPS MATERIAL TO: {source} links {target}'s cost to a specific deliverable in the breakdown structure
verbGlossary.GROUPS COSTS OF: {source} aggregates {target}'s costs for reporting and variance analysis
verbGlossary.TAGS PROCUREMENT OF: {source} labels {target} procurement transaction for budget tracking
verbGlossary.CONTRACTS LABOR OF: {source} formally engages {target}'s labor force for defined scope
verbGlossary.SCOPES LABOR OF: {source} defines the exact labor scope and unit pricing for {target}
verbGlossary.ITEMIZES QUANTITY OF: {source} breaks down {target} into specific quantities with unit costs
verbGlossary.DETAILS RENTAL OF: {source} specifies {target} rental terms, duration, and line-item cost
verbGlossary.ADDS SCOPE FOR: {source} introduces new work scope requiring {target} on the project
verbGlossary.REPRICES TONNAGE OF: {source} captures a price adjustment for {target} due to escalation or scope change
verbGlossary.RECOSTED FOR: {source} recalculates cost due to changed conditions affecting {target}'s work
verbGlossary.ADJUSTS QUANTITY OF: {source} modifies the {target} quantity from the original commitment
verbGlossary.PROPOSES EXTRA WORK FOR: {source} requests approval for additional work by {target}
verbGlossary.ESTIMATES ADDED COST OF: {source} quantifies the expected cost of additional {target} needed
verbGlossary.AUTHORIZES ADDITIONAL WORK BY: {source} formally approves extra scope performed by {target}
verbGlossary.COMMITS ADDED MATERIAL OF: {source} locks in the cost of additional {target} quantities
verbGlossary.BILLS ADDED LABOR OF: {target} charges for incremental labor hours {source} worked due to scope change
verbGlossary.PRICES EXTRA MATERIAL OF: {target} carries the unit price for additional {source} added via change order
verbGlossary.RECOVERS COST OF: {source} passes {target} cost increases through to the owner
verbGlossary.BILLS OWNER FOR: {target} charges the owner for added {source} work not in original scope
verbGlossary.TRANSFERS BUDGET TO: {source} moves dollars to cover {target} cost overrun
verbGlossary.POSTS DAY-RATE FOR: {source} posts a daily rate charge for {target} on time-and-materials basis
verbGlossary.RECORDS PURCHASE OF: {source} captures {target} purchase made outside the commitment process
verbGlossary.BOOKS RENTAL OF: {source} records {target} rental charge against the project
verbGlossary.VOUCHERS DELIVERY OF: {source} documents receipt and cost of {target} delivery
verbGlossary.INVOICES MOBILIZATION OF: {source} bills for the mobilization and setup of {target}
verbGlossary.DRAWS DOWN AGAINST: {source} draws against the contract value for {target}'s completed work
verbGlossary.BILLS INSTALLATION OF: {source} includes the installed value of {target} in the pay application
verbGlossary.PAYS PROGRESS OF: {source} pays for {target}'s work-in-place during the billing period
verbGlossary.COVERS INSTALLED VALUE OF: {source} reimburses the installed value of {target} in the work
verbGlossary.REIMBURSES STAND-BY OF: {target} charges for {source} kept on stand-by during delays
verbGlossary.WITHHOLDS PERCENT FROM: {target} holds back a contractual percentage from {source}'s earned payment
verbGlossary.HOLDS BACK ON: {source} defers a percentage of {target} payment until project completion
verbGlossary.RELEASES FUNDS TO: {source} disburses earned funds to {target} upon invoice approval
verbGlossary.SETTLES ACCOUNT FOR: {source} closes out the remaining balance owed for {target} procurement
verbGlossary.CLEARS LIEN ON: {source} releases lien rights on {target} delivered to the project
verbGlossary.BREAKS OUT COST OF: {source} itemizes {target}'s cost into billable line items
verbGlossary.LINE-ITEMS VALUE OF: {source} assigns a specific dollar value to {target} line for billing
verbGlossary.BUDGETS ALLOWANCE FOR: {target} sets aside a provisional dollar amount for {source} category
verbGlossary.BUFFERS OVERRUN ON: {target} provides a cost buffer to absorb labor overruns on {source}'s scope
verbGlossary.HEDGES ESCALATION OF: {source} protects against {target} price increases between budget and procurement
verbGlossary.PROJECTS SPEND ON: {source} estimates the total at-completion spend for {target}
verbGlossary.FORECASTS RENTAL OF: {source} predicts the cumulative rental cost for {target} through completion
verbGlossary.SCHEDULES PAYMENTS FOR: {source} times {target} payment milestones against the draw schedule
verbGlossary.FLAGS OVERRUN BY: {source} highlights when {target}'s actual costs exceed budgeted amount
verbGlossary.MEASURES ESCALATION OF: {source} quantifies how much {target}'s cost has increased
verbGlossary.BENCHMARKS PRODUCTIVITY OF: {source} compares earned value to actual cost for {target}'s output
verbGlossary.TRACKS OBLIGATION TO: {target} monitors total contractual dollars obligated to {source}
verbGlossary.TALLIES CHANGES FOR: {source} counts the frequency and dollar impact of changes for {target}
verbGlossary.DEPLETED BY OVERAGES ON: {source} tracks how {target} overruns consume the contingency reserve
verbGlossary.DELAYS PAYMENT FOR: {target} measures lag between {source}'s invoice and payment
verbGlossary.SIGNALS TIMING GAP ON: {source} identifies when actual {target} payment timing deviates from projection
verbGlossary.REVISES PROJECTION FOR: {source} updates the forecasted final cost for {target}

_meta.universalEdgeSemantics: Each universalEdge means: the source element connects to EVERY element in the targetGroup via the specified verb. targetGroup values: 'trades' (25 elements), 'equipment' (11 elements), 'materials' (25 elements), 'measures' (9 elements).
_meta.dataEvidence: Universal relationships validated via Databricks queries against prod_gold_ram_us01 (2-year lookback, March 2026). Q4: change events span 30 distinct reason codes. Financial instruments apply to all resource categories by definition — every trade, material, and equipment type has budget lines, cost codes, commitments, invoices, and change orders.
_meta.edgesVsUniversal: The 'edges' array contains the few truly SELECTIVE relationships — specific fee types (CXA allowance), equipment-specific metrics (crane cash flow variance), and stand-by charges.
